title: Registry-Path Reconciliation
description: Reconciliation between governance claims (registry) and governance observations (path map). Registry declares what an object IS. Path map observes where an object ROUTES. Inconsistency = governance drift, dispatched via A1-A4. Reconciled ≠ Synchronized.
core_invariants: Observed Drift ≠ Failure — it's a governance finding

RPR-1.name: REGISTRY_ENTRY_FILE_MISSING
RPR-1.meaning: Registry claims file exists but git has no such file

RPR-2.name: FILE_EXISTS_WITHOUT_REGISTRY
RPR-2.meaning: Governed path has file but registry has no claim

RPR-3.name: DOC_TYPE_ROUTE_MISMATCH
RPR-3.meaning: Registry doc_type conflicts with path-map route

RPR-4.name: AUTHORITY_ROUTE_RISK_MISMATCH
RPR-4.meaning: Registry authority conflicts with path risk level

RPR-5.name: OWNER_CLAIM_UNSUPPORTED
RPR-5.meaning: Registry claims owner but no ownership evidence

RPR-6.name: GENERATED_VIEW_AUTHORITY_MISMATCH
RPR-6.meaning: Generated view claimed as source/current truth

RPR-7.name: EXCLUSION_CONFLICT
RPR-7.meaning: Registry claims governance but path map excludes

RPR-8.name: PATH_RULE_GAP
RPR-8.meaning: File exists, registry has claim, but no path-map rule covers it

RPR-9.name: INTENTIONAL_GAP_REQUIRES_DEBT
RPR-9.meaning: Neither registry nor path map covers file — debt required

RPR-10.name: OBSERVATION_OVERRIDES_CLAIM
RPR-10.meaning: Path map independently owns metadata that should derive from registry

disposition_routing.A1: Fix registry claim — wrong owner, doc_type, authority, lifecycle
disposition_routing.A2: Fix path-map rule — new doc_type not covered, route too narrow
disposition_routing.A3: Fix governance model — gap between authority tiers and path risk levels
disposition_routing.A4: Record formal debt — intentional gap, legacy, external, not yet governable
